macro_summary: Rate backdrop (FRED, not live): payrolls +57k (cooling from +129k); unemployment 4.2% (down); last CPI 4.3% YoY (up, stale). US-Iran hostilities escalated over the weekend after President Trump ended the peace framework, with US airstrikes hitting Iranian energy infrastructure and Tehran maintaining its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, sending WTI crude surging 4.2% to $74.38. The renewed tensions overshadowed Friday's modest gains on Wall Street, pushing Asian markets broadly lower — Korean memory stocks slumped as SK Hynix shed 14% in Seoul after its record Nasdaq debut (per Reuters, Jul 13). Markets now await Tuesday's CPI report and earnings from ASML and TSMC later in the week.
commodities_note: Oil surged as Hormuz traffic remains near standstill, while gold retreated 1.1% to $4,068 on firmer yields after the Fed acknowledged stepped-up inflation in its report to Congress (per investingLive, Jul 10). The US dollar edged higher.

NVTS.stale_session_note: No new session since 2026-07-10 — bar date and price identical to the previous report (market closed: holiday/weekend). Technicals are unchanged, already-reported data.

pre_earnings_band.setup_rationale: vs_sma50 -35.9%, RSI 34 — standard expectations game; reaction depends on guidance delta.

writeup.headline: Promoted to AVOID — Wolfspeed patent suit threatens Navitas AI chip business.
writeup.what_to_do: Promoted to AVOID — Wolfspeed's patent lawsuit (per MarketBeat, Jul 11) seeks an import injunction on five products, a direct threat to Navitas' AI data-center chip business. The stock is already down 41% in a month; this legal risk adds unquantifiable downside. Avoid until the suit is resolved or key products are cleared for sale. Relative strength: lagging AI Power Infrastructure by 27.7% over the month.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if NVTS Jul 27 earnings miss by >5% or guidance is cut.

avoid_source.headline_fragment: Wolfspeed sued Navitas Semiconductor over five wide-bandgap patents, seeking an import injunction

writeup.headline: PLTR defense thesis solid, but 60x PE leaves no room for error.
writeup.what_to_do: Palantir's defense AI thesis is strong — Maven is a program of record, $10B Army EA executing — but the 60x forward PE and 146x EV/EBITDA leave no room for error. The 18.2:1 headline R:R is distorted by a 0.4% stop; the actionable stop at $122.68 yields 2.3:1. Wait for a pullback to $122.68 for that entry, or clarity from Q2 earnings on Aug 3.
writeup.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 60x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if multiple compresses toward the BigTech cluster median (24.6x) without a corresponding earnings re-rating.

WRD.stale_session_note: No new session since 2026-07-10 — bar date and price identical to the previous report (market closed: holiday/weekend). Technicals are unchanged, already-reported data.
thesis_highlights: WeRide bull case rests on B2B fleet licensing (Uber, Grab, Bosch) + multi-country Robotaxi deployment — evaluate on commercial traction, not autonomy benchmarks vs Waymo/Tesla.
ADR/China delisting risk is a standing consideration — always maintain starter sizing for WRD.

writeup.headline: WeRide grinding toward $5.18 support — B2B fleet story provides floor.
writeup.what_to_do: WeRide is grinding lower at $5.57, approaching support at $5.18. The B2B fleet licensing story (Uber, Grab) provides a long-term option, but ADR delisting risk caps upside. A bounce from $5.18 with volume would be a tactical entry; otherwise, wait for a catalyst.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if WRD fails to reclaim SMA50 ($6.74) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

writeup.headline: SiTime -9.5% below 50-day, but 51x PE offers no valuation floor.
writeup.what_to_do: SiTime is 9.5% below its 50-day, but the forward PE of 51x and PEG of 3.8 leave no valuation support. The MEMS timing thesis is real, but the stock hasn't found a floor. Reassess on a bounce off $609.15 support or after Aug 6 earnings.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks are blisteringly extended — all three sit 11-14% above their 50-day SMAs with RSIs above 80. The rally is divorced from the NIM-compression reality (US10Y 4.57%, MAS S$NEER unchanged). UOB is the cheapest at 12x forward, but the cluster needs a pullback to the 50-day averages before any entry.
singapore.key_development: DBS, OCBC, and UOB all posted fresh 52-week highs last week on no bank-specific news, driven by broad SGD strength.

semiconductors.summary: The Semis cluster remains split: while NVDA reclaims its 50-day and executive commentary insists AI demand is 'almost unlimited' (per CNBC), the memory complex sold off sharply as SK Hynix fell 14% in Seoul post-Nasdaq debut. Multiple names (AMD, AVGO, LITE, SNDK) are hard-blocked on extension or momentum. The cluster is wealthy in thesis but poor in entry points — wait for consolidation.
semiconductors.key_development: SK Hynix's 14% single-day drop — the largest in the cluster — is profit-taking after the $26.5B Nasdaq IPO, not a fundamental signal.

big_tech.summary: BigTech remains subdued below their 50-day averages, weighed by the $350B AI debt pile (per Bloomberg) and the lingering OpenAI credit-circularity question at MSFT. GOOG reports in 9 days — the TPU-vs-GPU debate is the key read — while AMZN and MSFT await late-month earnings. No entry before the binary events.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautious — capex confirmed, but ROI validation pending.
big_tech.key_development: Bloomberg reported that the five biggest data-center spenders have doubled their debt to $350B over five years, raising fresh questions about AI capex returns.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received NVDA's matching $2B photonics investment alongside LITE — co-packaged optics read-through for AI datacenter interconnects.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's Aug 18 earnings confirm >$1B optics revenue run-rate, validating the CPO adoption cycle.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Shipped 5M photonic ICs via Tower Semiconductor (per Simply Wall St., Jul 10), positioning as a leader in AI data-center optics; competes with Broadcom in custom ASICs.
interconnected.entry_note: Buyable on a pullback to the 50-day if the photonics revenue acceleration continues and custom-silicon wins broaden.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking switch vendor; direct beneficiary of hyperscaler capex and NVDA Spectrum-X ecosystem.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if ANET guides above $8B in Q2 print, confirming the 800G switch upgrade cycle.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz conflict — US airstrikes target Iranian energy sites, Strait of Hormuz blockaded, oil surge.
China helium export ban — threatens semiconductor manufacturing gas supply amid US-Iran tensions (per Reuters, Jul 10).
Fed hawkish pivot — Fed officials openly weigh rate hikes as inflation fears persist; June CPI print on Tuesday is the next catalyst.
US-China trade/tariff tensions — India rejects quick trade deal (per Reuters, Jul 13); USMCA stalemate; Section 122 tariff surcharge in place.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil surge from Hormuz re-kindles energy-inflation risk, pressuring rate-sensitive semis and boosting Singapore bank yields appeal. The helium ban adds a wildcard for chip manufacturing. SK Hynix 14% drop in Seoul is a symptom of Asian risk-off.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump ended the Iran peace framework; US airstrikes targeted Iran's energy infrastructure; Hormuz blockade remains. Oil surged 4.2% to $74.38. India rejected a quick US trade deal.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate persists with oil near $75; inflation fears keep the Fed hawkish, capping semis. Singapore banks benefit from higher rates but remain overbought. Asia remains under pressure.

optimistic.description: A ceasefire is reached, oil drops below $60, easing inflation and allowing the Fed to pause; semis rally as NVDA and TSM earnings validate AI demand.

pessimistic.description: Full-blown conflict disrupts global oil supply, WTI surges above $100, recession fears crush tech multiples; AVGO, AMD, LITE fall below their 200-day averages.

wildcard.description: China leverages the helium ban to pressure US, triggering broader tech-supply disruption; ASML and Micron face production headwinds.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold current positions; no new entries until the CPI report Tuesday provides clarity on the inflation trajectory. Selective entry on oversold memory (SK Hynix, Samsung) if they stabilize.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 7 of 31 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, but the broader risk is the Hormuz-driven oil shock, which could re-ignite inflation and force the Fed to hike. Watch CPI tomorrow.
